title: Richly Fermented Uruguayan Squid Ceviche
description: A sour and juicy Uruguayan dish featuring squid, perfect for a weeknight dinner, with Mediterranean-friendly flavors and a rich fermentation process.

ingredients.name: squid
ingredients.notes: cleaned and cut into bite-sized pieces

instructions: In a large bowl, combine squid, lime juice, lemon juice, garlic, onion, salt, black pepper, and oregano.
Mix well to combine, making sure the squid is completely coated with the marinade.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight to allow the squid to ferment.
After the fermentation process, remove the bowl from the refrigerator and stir in the olive oil.
Add the bay leaves to the bowl and stir to combine.
Let the mixture sit at room temperature for 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.
Just before serving, remove the bay leaves and discard.
Serve the ceviche chilled, garnished with additional lime wedges if desired.
